_comment: English → Japanese | Month 1 Week 1 | Pronunciation Practice 2
title: Pronunciation Practice 2: Ka-row & Sa-row Hiragana
description: Practice the ka-row (か行) and sa-row (さ行) Hiragana consonants.

blocks.title: Ka-row and Sa-row Hiragana
points: か(ka) き(ki) く(ku) け(ke) こ(ko) — k + vowel
さ(sa) し(shi) す(su) せ(se) そ(so) — s + vowel
Important: し is 'shi' NOT 'si' — the mouth positions differently
す (su) — the 'u' is very soft, almost silent in normal speech
These are among the most common characters in Japanese!

blocks.title: Identify the Sound Difference
blocks.instruction: Notice the subtle difference in these pairs:

pairs.diff: Dakuten (゛) makes the consonant voiced: か→が, さ→ざ, た→だ

words.note: Cherry blossom — Japan's national flower! sa-ku-ra, all even

items.explanation: No! 'し' is pronounced 'shi' — like 'she' in English.

items.statement: Adding dakuten (゛) to a character makes it voiced.
items.explanation: Correct! か (ka) → が (ga), さ (sa) → ざ (za).

items.statement: All syllables in Japanese have equal stress.
items.explanation: Yes! Japanese has pitch accent but no strong stress accent — all morae are relatively equal.
